records.ety: [L. litigiosus, fr. litigium dispute, quarrel, fr. litigare: cf. F. litigieux. See Litigation.]

defs: Inclined to initiate lawsuits; given to the practice of contending in law; fond of litigation.
quotes: Soldiers find wars, and lawyers find out still Litigious men, who quarrels move.

defs: Subject to contention; disputable; controvertible; debatable; doubtful; precarious.
quotes: No fences, parted fields, nor marks, nor bounds, Distinguished acres of litigious grounds.

defs: Of or pertaining to legal disputes.
quotes: Nor brothers cite to the litigious bar.
